# Sprint Change Proposal — 2026-04-23

**Trigger:** Autonomous drift detection — "Drift detected. Review requirements."
**Generated by:** bmad-correct-course workflow (autonomous mode)
**Project:** EU Solicit
**Scope Classification:** Moderate — backlog reorganisation + standards update; no PM/Architect replan required

---

## Section 1: Issue Summary

### Problem Statement

A systematic **review-pipeline bottleneck** has been identified across the sprint. As of 2026-04-23, **43 stories across 11 active epics (E02–E13)** are stuck in `review` state and have never been transitioned to `done`. A further **15 stories** sit at `ready-for-dev` or `in-progress`. No epic beyond E01 has been closed.

This drift compounds with three recurring critical action items that have been carry-forwards for **6–7 consecutive epics** without resolution:
- k6 performance baseline — 6th carry-forward (E03 → E05 → E06 → E07 → E08 → now)
- Dependabot configuration — 6th carry-forward (E01 → E02 → E05 → E06 → E07 → E08)
- TEA test review (≥80/100 per story) — 7th consecutive epic with zero coverage

### Discovery Context

Evidence gathered from:
- `eusolicit-docs/implementation-artifacts/sprint-status.yaml` — status snapshot as of 2026-04-23
- `eusolicit-docs/planning-artifacts/project-context.md` — accumulated patterns and anti-patterns (E04–E10)
- `eusolicit-docs/implementation-artifacts/deferred-work.md` — 4 deferred work items with story files (dw-01, dw-02, dw-03, 7-17)
- `eusolicit-docs/planning-artifacts/implementation-readiness-report-2026-04-22.md` — FR coverage 163/163; UX gaps noted

## Section 2: Impact Analysis

### 2.1 Epic Impact

| Epic | Completability | Impact |
|------|---------------|--------|
| E02  | Blocked by 2 `review` stories | 2-4 (JWT middleware) and 2-11 (audit trail) have been in review since E02 execution. |
| E03  | Blocked by 2 `review` stories | 3-2 (Tailwind theming) and 3-10 (loading states) are early frontend foundation stories. |
| E04  | Blocked by 3 `review` + 1 `ready-for-dev` | AI Gateway is functionally operational; 3 stories awaiting review + 1 (rate limiting) never started. |
| E05  | Blocked by 1 `review` (5-5 TED crawler) | One crawler task outstanding; all others done. |
| E06  | Blocked by 1 `review` + 1 `ready-for-dev` | 6-5 (opportunity detail API) was a P0 Sprint 7 carry-forward; still at `ready-for-dev`. |
| E07  | Blocked by 7 `review` stories | The bulk of the proposal workspace frontend is in review. These block E07 closure. |
| E08  | Blocked by 13 `review` stories | All billing stories except 8-14 are in review. Tier cache invalidation alone is `done`. |
| E09  | Blocked by 8 `review` + 1 `ready-for-dev` + 1 `in-progress` | Notification service partially operational (9-11, 9-12, 9-13, 9-14 done). |
| E10  | 8 stories at `ready-for-dev` (never started) | Collaboration/RBAC stories were deprioritised. 4 stories done (comments, tasks). |
| E11  | 3 `review` + 1 `in-progress` | Grants & compliance mostly done; ESPD migration still in-progress. |
| E12  | 2 `review` + 1 `ready-for-dev` | Analytics platform mostly done; reporting delivery, admin frontend, forecasting dashboard outstanding. |
| E13  | 1 `review` + 3 `ready-for-dev` | Cleanup sprint: security hardening (7-17) awaiting review; DW stories need implementation. |

### 2.2 Story-Level Impact

**Oldest blocking stories (highest remediation priority):**
1. `2-4-jwt-authentication-middleware` (review) — Auth foundation; 4+ epics old
2. `2-11-audit-trail-middleware` (review) — Audit compliance foundation; 4+ epics old
3. `3-2-tailwind-design-token-preset-shadcn-ui-theming` (review) — Frontend design system base
4. `4-9-rate-limit-management-and-concurrency-control` (ready-for-dev) — Never implemented; AI Gateway rate limiting

**Critical functional gaps (never implemented):**
- `6-5-opportunity-detail-api-endpoint` (ready-for-dev) — Blocks proposal context wiring
- `10-1` through `10-3`, `10-8`, `10-10`, `10-11`, `10-14`, `10-16` — All E10 collaboration features at `ready-for-dev`; RBAC, section locking, approval workflows never implemented

**Epic 8 (Billing) — all 13 stories in review:**
The entire billing module (Stripe provisioning, subscriptions, webhooks, usage metering, VAT, invoicing) has been implemented but never code-reviewed and closed. This is a revenue-critical path with no confirmed `done` status.

### 2.3 Artifact Conflicts

| Artifact | Status | Conflict |
|----------|--------|----------|
| PRD (`EU_Solicit_PRD_v2.md`) | ✓ No conflict | 163/163 FRs covered; MVP scope intact |
| `epics.md` planning document | ⚠️ Structural mismatch | Describes 8 user-value epics; actual implementation used 12 technical epics + E13. Not a conflict—this was intentional evolution—but the planning document is outdated |
| Architecture (`EU_Solicit_Solution_Architecture_v5.md`) | ✓ No conflict | Architecture is sound and correctly implemented |
| UX Design | ⚠️ Minor gaps | Keyboard navigation, responsive data table, StatusBadge coverage noted as missing in readiness report; non-blocking |
| `sprint-status.yaml` | ❌ Drift | Reflects current state correctly but reveals bottleneck; needs no correction—it IS the source of truth |
| `deferred-work.md` | ✓ Accurate | Accurately lists all deferred items; three have story files (dw-01, dw-02, dw-03) |

### 2.4 Technical Impact

**k6 performance baseline absent (6th carry-forward):**
PRD SLAs (p95 <200ms REST, <500ms SSE TTFB) are unverifiable. `load-test-results.md` is an unfilled template. This affects E06 (SSE opportunity streaming), E07 (SSE proposal generation), E08 (usage metering concurrency), E12 (analytics materialized views).

**Dependabot not configured (6th carry-forward):**
New packages added across 6 epics (WeasyPrint, python-docx, tiptap/*, recharts, Stripe SDK, VIES client, pyclamd, boto3, testcontainers) are unscanned. Any CVE in these packages is invisible.

**TEA test review at 0% (7th consecutive epic):**
No story has a TEA review score ≥80/100. Backend/frontend contract drift (E07: `ProposalResponse` missing fields, `useBreakpoint` threshold mismatch) is only caught by TEA. Without TEA, specification-vs-implementation gaps compound.

---

## Section 3: Recommended Approach

### Selected Path: Option 1 — Direct Adjustment (Incremental Review Blitz + Injected Hardening Stories)

**Rationale:** The MVP is functionally complete at the code level. The pipeline stall is a *process* problem (reviews not being executed), not a requirements or architecture problem. Rolling back (Option 2) or narrowing MVP scope (Option 3) would destroy delivered value. The correct action is to systematically process the review backlog in priority order, inject three previously-deferred stories, and permanently close the k6/Dependabot gaps before any new feature development.

**Effort:** Medium — 43 code reviews + 3 implementations + 3 injected stories
**Risk:** Low — no architectural changes; only review completions and small well-scoped implementations
**Timeline impact:** 1–2 additional sprint iterations to clear review backlog before Epic 14 can begin

### Recommended Priority Sequence

**Phase 1 — Clear Ancient Review Debt (E02–E06): Stories frozen for 4+ epics**
1. `2-4-jwt-authentication-middleware` → review → done
2. `2-11-audit-trail-middleware` → review → done
3. `3-2-tailwind-design-token-preset-shadcn-ui-theming` → review → done
4. `3-10-loading-states-error-boundaries-empty-states` → review → done
5. `4-2`, `4-3`, `4-8` → review → done
6. Implement `4-9-rate-limit-management-and-concurrency-control` (ready-for-dev → done)
7. `5-5-ted-crawler-task` → review → done
8. `6-12-document-upload-component` → review → done
9. Implement `6-5-opportunity-detail-api-endpoint` (P0 carry-forward)

**Phase 2 — Clear E07 Proposal Workspace Reviews**
10. `7-6, 7-7, 7-12, 7-13, 7-14, 7-15, 7-16` → review → done

**Phase 3 — Clear E08 Billing Reviews (revenue-critical)**
11. All 13 billing stories (`8-1` through `8-13`) → review → done

**Phase 4 — Clear E09–E12 Reviews + Implement DW Stories**
12. E09 review stories (8 stories) → done
13. `9-5-daily-weekly-digest-assembly` → in-progress → done
14. `11-1-espd-profile-compliance-framework-db-schema-migrations` → in-progress → done
15. E11 review stories (3 stories) → done
16. E12 review stories + `12-7` → done
17. Implement `dw-01`, `dw-02`, `dw-03` (E13 DW stories)
18. `7-17-proposal-generate-security-audit-hardening` → review → done

**Phase 5 — Inject Three Mandatory Hardening Stories (new stories in E13)**
19. **INJ-01**: `dependabot-configuration` — Create `.github/dependabot.yml` for Python + JS packages. Points: 1. Blocks: all future epics. (6th carry-forward; must not become 7th)
20. **INJ-02**: `k6-performance-baseline` — Write and execute k6 scripts for REST p95 <200ms and SSE TTFB <500ms; commit results to `load-test-results.md`. Points: 3. (6th carry-forward)
21. **INJ-03**: `tea-review-backlog-epic8-epic9` — Execute TEA scored reviews (≥80/100) for minimum 3 stories from E08 (billing) and 3 from E09 (notifications). Points: 2.

**Phase 6 — E10 Collaboration (8 ready-for-dev stories)**
22. Implement E10 stories in order: `10-1 → 10-2 → 10-3 → 10-8 → 10-9(done) → 10-10 → 10-11 → 10-14 → 10-16`

## Section 4: Detailed Change Proposals

### 4.1 Sprint-Status Changes

#### Epic Closure Gates (apply after Phase completions)

```yaml
# After Phase 1 complete:
epic-2: done   # 2-4 and 2-11 reviewed and closed
epic-3: done   # 3-2 and 3-10 reviewed and closed
epic-4: done   # all 10 stories done (including 4-9 implemented)
epic-5: done   # 5-5 reviewed and closed
epic-6: done   # 6-5 implemented, 6-12 reviewed and closed

# After Phase 2 complete:
epic-7: done   # all 7 review stories closed + 7-17 in E13

# After Phase 3 complete:
epic-8: done   # all 13 billing stories reviewed and closed

# After Phase 4 complete:
epic-9: done   # all outstanding stories closed
epic-11: done  # 11-1 completed, review stories closed
epic-12: done  # 12-7, 12-10, 12-14 closed
```

#### New Story Entries for E13 (inject before Phase 5)

```yaml
# Add to epic-13 section in sprint-status.yaml:
dw-01-proposal-backend-schema-alignment: ready-for-dev  # already present
dw-02-celery-task-infrastructure-fixes: ready-for-dev   # already present
dw-03-ui-breakpoint-hook-fix: ready-for-dev             # already present
7-17-proposal-generate-security-audit-hardening: review # already present
inj-01-dependabot-configuration: backlog                # NEW
inj-02-k6-performance-baseline: backlog                 # NEW
inj-03-tea-review-backlog-epic8-epic9: backlog          # NEW
```

### 4.2 Story Changes

#### Story: `6-5-opportunity-detail-api-endpoint`
**Status change:** `ready-for-dev` → implement as P0 (same AC as original S06.05 from E06 epic file)
**Note:** This story blocks proposal context wiring in E07 and was marked as a P0 Sprint 7 carry-forward in E06 retrospective. Must be completed before E07 can be fully declared `done`.

#### New Story: `INJ-01 — dependabot-configuration`

```
Story: INJ-01 Dependabot Configuration
Section: Full Story

As a platform operator,
I want Dependabot configured for all Python and JavaScript packages,
so that known CVEs in any dependency are automatically surfaced as PRs.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. `.github/dependabot.yml` created with:
   - Python pip: weekly schedule, all 5 services + packages
   - npm/pnpm: weekly schedule, all frontend apps and packages
   - GitHub Actions: monthly schedule
2. File merged to main branch (no open PRs required, only config committed)
3. First Dependabot scan initiated (no failures required, only activation)

Tasks:
- [ ] Create .github/dependabot.yml
- [ ] Configure pip ecosystem for services/client-api, admin-api, data-pipeline, ai-gateway, notification
- [ ] Configure npm ecosystem for frontend/apps/client, frontend/apps/admin, frontend/packages/ui
- [ ] PR + merge
```

#### New Story: `INJ-02 — k6-performance-baseline`

```
Story: INJ-02 k6 Performance Baseline
Section: Full Story

As a platform operator,
I want k6 load test scripts written, executed, and committed,
so that PRD SLAs (p95 <200ms REST, <500ms SSE TTFB) are verifiable.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. k6 script: REST p95 latency for GET /opportunities (search with FTS) — must pass <200ms at 50 VUs
2. k6 script: SSE TTFB for POST /opportunities/:id/summary (AI summary stream) — first byte <500ms at 10 VUs
3. k6 script: Redis usage metering counter under 100 concurrent INCR operations
4. Results written to eusolicit-docs/implementation-artifacts/load-test-results.md
5. Scripts committed to eusolicit-app/tests/load/

Tasks:
- [ ] Create tests/load/ directory
- [ ] Write k6 script for REST search (k6 run with thresholds)
- [ ] Write k6 script for SSE TTFB (k6 experimental streams or http.get + check timing)
- [ ] Write k6 script for Redis metering
- [ ] Execute against running services (make up)
- [ ] Fill load-test-results.md with actual p50/p95/p99 values
- [ ] Commit all scripts
```

#### New Story: `INJ-03 — tea-review-backlog-epic8-epic9`

```
Story: INJ-03 TEA Review Backlog (E08 + E09)
Section: Full Story

As a quality gate enforcer,
I want TEA scored reviews (≥80/100) completed for 6 high-risk stories,
so that the 7-epic TEA gap is partially closed and the billing + notification
paths have independent quality validation.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. TEA review for S08.04 (stripe-webhook) — score ≥80/100, findings documented
2. TEA review for S08.08 (usage-metering-redis-counters) — score ≥80/100
3. TEA review for S08.10 (eu-vat-vies-validation) — score ≥80/100
4. TEA review for S09.04 (alert-matching-immediate-dispatch) — score ≥80/100
5. TEA review for S09.06 (sendgrid-email-delivery) — score ≥80/100
6. TEA review for S09.08 (google-calendar-oauth2-sync) — score ≥80/100
7. All review artifacts saved to eusolicit-docs/implementation-artifacts/tea-reviews/

Tasks:
- [ ] Run bmad-tea on 08-4-stripe-webhook-endpoint-subscription-lifecycle-sync.md
- [ ] Run bmad-tea on 08-8-usage-metering-with-redis-counters-stripe-sync.md
- [ ] Run bmad-tea on 08-10-eu-vat-handling-via-stripe-tax-vies-validation.md
- [ ] Run bmad-tea on 09-4-alert-matching-immediate-dispatch.md
- [ ] Run bmad-tea on 09-6-sendgrid-email-delivery-template-management.md
- [ ] Run bmad-tea on 09-8-google-calendar-oauth2-sync.md
- [ ] Save all review artifacts
```

## Section 5: Implementation Handoff

### Scope Classification: **Moderate**
> No PM or Architect involvement required. Stories are well-specified. Changes are limited to clearing the review pipeline, implementing 4 small deferred stories, injecting 3 hardening stories, and closing completed epics.

### Handoff: Developer Agent

**Primary responsibilities:**
1. Execute code reviews for all 43 `review`-state stories in the priority order defined in Section 3
2. Implement `6-5`, `4-9`, `dw-01`, `dw-02`, `dw-03` (5 stories at `ready-for-dev`)
3. Create story files for INJ-01, INJ-02, INJ-03 and implement them
4. Transition epic statuses to `done` as each phase completes (per closure gates in §4.1)
5. Do NOT start any new epic (E14+) until review backlog is cleared through Phase 4

**Secondary responsibilities (Product Owner coordination):**
- Confirm E10 collaboration stories (10-1 through 10-16) are still in MVP scope before Phase 6
- These 8 stories have never been started; confirm priority before Developer agent begins them

### Success Criteria

| Gate | Criterion |
|------|-----------|
| Phase 1 complete | Epics 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 all show `done` in sprint-status.yaml |
| Phase 2 complete | Epic 7 shows `done` |
| Phase 3 complete | Epic 8 shows `done`; Stripe webhook integration has confirmed `done` status |
| Phase 4 complete | Epics 9, 11, 12 show `done`; E13 DW stories done |
| INJ-01 done | `.github/dependabot.yml` merged; no future "Dependabot not configured" anti-pattern |
| INJ-02 done | `load-test-results.md` contains actual p50/p95 values; no future "k6 absent" anti-pattern |
| INJ-03 done | 6 TEA review artifacts exist with scores ≥80/100 |
| Phase 6 (if approved) | E10 RBAC, section locking, approval workflows implemented and `done` |
